assumption_text: The Chernihiv episode uses the city municipality and immediately contiguous defended approaches, not all of Chernihiv Oblast.
rationale: Civilian harm and population evidence located in the repair pass are city-bounded; whole-oblast denominators would dilute exposure.

assumption_text: Civilian person-months are modeled from the official 1 January 2022 city population and an end-March remaining-population anchor of about 130,000, with low/best/high scenarios varying the timing of outflow.
rationale: A pre-war stock and a late-siege anchor exist, but no daily population series was located.

assumption_text: Average Ukrainian defender presence is modeled at roughly 2.5k / 4k / 6k personnel over the 40-day siege.
rationale: Sources identify the 1st Tank Brigade and note reinforcement by territorial-defence and reservist elements, but no city-only strength time series was found.

assumption_text: The 70 unidentified dead in the mayoral Chernihiv total are kept as unknown-status deaths and excluded from the core DR rather than allocated to civilians or military personnel.
rationale: This avoids silently assigning status when the source does not specify it.

assumption_text: The mayoral figure of about 700 dead is treated as a contextual battle-period city total and used only to derive a conservative military residual.
rationale: No stronger city-specific defender fatality series surfaced in the repair pass.

assumption_text: The siege exposure window is approximated as 40 days (2022-02-24 to 2022-04-04) for person-month conversion.
rationale: Operational sources place lifting of the siege on 31 March and nearly complete oblast recapture by 6 April.

assumption_text: The Kyiv northwest cluster should eventually be clipped to occupied/contested settlements on the Bucha-Irpin-Hostomel axis rather than all of Kyiv Oblast or all of OHCHR's three-region occupation report.
rationale: Available UN evidence is broader than the intended cluster footprint.

assumption_text: Mariupol burial and excess-mortality estimates are excluded from the core direct-death path unless a later pass cleanly separates civilians from military personnel and direct from non-direct deaths.
rationale: The investigative report explicitly says those figures do not provide that separation.

assumption_text: Kharkiv claims framed as casualties or formation losses are not converted into deaths unless the source explicitly provides fatalities.
rationale: The repair pass found multiple casualty-heavy military sources but too few clean death-only series.

assumption_text: Bakhmut statements that combine killed and severely wounded are stored as casualty context and not converted into deaths.
rationale: The source wording does not support a direct-death extraction.
